qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Algorithm confusion in JWS verification allows signature bypass
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Shares the JWS verification surface, but identifies an HS256/Data-key algorithm-confusion path through prepareJWK and HMAC verification, not the advisory's alg:none early return that accepts unsigned tokens.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: PBES2 accepts unbounded iteration count causing denial-of-service via CPU exhaustion
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable component and root cause: this finding concerns attacker-controlled PBES2 p2c iteration counts in JWE decryption, while the advisory concerns JWS/JWT verification accepting alg:none without cryptographic verification.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying final finding identifies the advisory-described alg:none verification bypass in JWS.verify/JWT.verify. The only JWS qualifying finding is a separate HS256 key-confusion issue, and the other qualifying finding is a PBES2 JWE denial-of-service issue.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-88q6-jcjg-hvmw describes Sources/JSONWebSignature/JWS+Verify.swift lines 34-37 returning true when protectedHeader.algorithm is SigningAlgorithm.none, without checking the signature, key, or explicit caller opt-in.

algorithm_confusion_jws/vulnerability_report.json describes prepareJWK trusting an attacker-controlled HS256 header when the verifier key is raw Data, bypassing the algorithm consistency check because the manufactured JWK has no algorithm field, and then using the supplied bytes as an HMAC secret.
pbes2_iteration_dos/vulnerability_report.json describes unbounded PBES2 p2c iteration counts reaching PBKDF2 during JWE decryption, which is unrelated to JWS/JWT alg:none signature verification.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding was present, so PoC execution was not applicable under the decision order.
